# Last Coin Standing

> Bitcoin education through interactive visualizations and rigorous editorial analysis. The site examines sound money, monetary tradeoffs, and Bitcoin's irreducible design through contrast and clarity — combining long-form arguments with interactive calculators that let readers stress-test the claims against historical data.

Each page is a self-contained exploration. The site does not provide financial advice; it provides frameworks, data, and tools so readers can think through Bitcoin decisions themselves.

## Start Here

The orientation pathway for newcomers — a curated entry point into the site.

- [Start Here](https://lastcoinstanding.com/start-here): A curated path for people relatively new to Bitcoin. Seven explorations sequenced so each one earns the next: framework (What Money Has To Be) → emotion (Melting Ice Cube) → quantification (Half-Life) → introduction (Bitcoin Defined) → mechanism (Synthesis) → evidence (Power Law + Gallery as paired step) → commitment (Bitcoin Migration). By the seventh, the reader understands the structural case for Bitcoin more clearly than most people who hold it. Closes with an applied-next-steps block highlighting The Bitcoin Retirement and Bitcoin vs. Real Estate.

## Foundations

Conceptual underpinnings of money and Bitcoin.

- [What Money Has To Be](https://lastcoinstanding.com/what-money-has-to-be): The three structurally-coupled functions of money — hold value across time, move across space, serve as a shared unit of measurement. Interactive comparison of bitcoin, gold, the US dollar, and a hyperinflating fiat.
- [What Money Is For](https://lastcoinstanding.com/what-money-is-for): The three purposes of money — save, invest, consume — and how each money holds or fails to hold them. Saving is the default state; investing and consuming are the two choices that flow from it. Sister page to What Money Has To Be (properties vs. purposes). Interactive comparison of bitcoin, gold, the US dollar, and a hyperinflating fiat.
- [What Bitcoin Is](https://lastcoinstanding.com/what-bitcoin-is): Bitcoin's core monetary properties — scarcity, decentralization, immutability, censorship-resistance — visualized as interlocking petals.
- [Bitcoin Defined](https://lastcoinstanding.com/bitcoin-defined): The single-sentence definition. Bitcoin is the open, permissionless, decentralized, secure protocol — bounded by energy, absolutely scarce. Eight load-bearing ideas, unpacked one at a time. The shortest accurate description of Bitcoin available.
- [The Bitcoin Synthesis](https://lastcoinstanding.com/synthesis): Six existing ideas combined into one irreducible monetary discovery. Explore each component, then remove one to see what collapses.
- [The Bitcoin Trilemma](https://lastcoinstanding.com/trilemma): The decentralization / security / scalability tradeoff. Why Bitcoin deliberately sacrifices base-layer speed for the other two.

## The Arguments

Editorial essays on Bitcoin's role as the monetary endgame.

- [The Bitcoin Migration](https://lastcoinstanding.com/the-bitcoin-migration): The exodus from an inherently broken monetary network that can't be fixed, to an inherently strong one that can't be broken. A long-form exploration of what money is, why fiat fails, and why Bitcoin is the answer.
- [The Half-Life](https://lastcoinstanding.com/the-half-life): How long until cash loses half its value? Interactive decay curve under different inflation assumptions, then a comparison of supply mechanics across fiat, gold, and Bitcoin.
- [Money Trees](https://lastcoinstanding.com/money-trees): Sound money and fiat money as two different root systems with different outcomes — fixed supply versus infinite expansion.
- [The Melting Ice Cube](https://lastcoinstanding.com/the-melting-ice-cube): Holding cash isn't conservatism — it's an active decision with a quantifiable ongoing cost. Interactive treasury model.
- [Is Bitcoin a Bubble?](https://lastcoinstanding.com/not-a-bubble): Every historical bubble followed a pattern of collapse to permanent zero. Bitcoin has crashed 80%+ three times and reached new all-time highs each time. The bubble checklist, applied honestly.
- [Bitcoin Spend and Replace](https://lastcoinstanding.com/spend-and-replace): What spending bitcoin and immediately repurchasing it actually buys you under US federal tax law in 2026. Debunks the "net-zero balance" illusion, distinguishes "Spend then Replace" from "Replace then Spend," and lays out the practical tactics — HIFO standing orders, wallet-by-wallet basis tracking, Notice 2026-20 books-and-records relief, the 1099-DA zero-basis trap — for executing the sequence cleanly. Covers the legislative horizon including the Lummis-Gillibrand proposal and the Digital Asset PARITY Act.

## The Numbers

Data-driven analyses with interactive calculators. Backed by historical price data, transparent math, and explicit assumptions.

- [Bitcoin and The Power Law](https://lastcoinstanding.com/the-power-law): The empirical law governing Bitcoin's price scaling — for every ~13% increase in Bitcoin's age, the trend price doubles. The structural reference for reading entry quality.
- [Bitcoin vs. The Stock Market](https://lastcoinstanding.com/bitcoin-vs-the-stock-market): Bitcoin against the S&P 500 and NASDAQ-100 across every monthly entry from 2010 and every common holding horizon. 100% win rate at 7+ year horizons. Includes an interactive outperformance heatmap covering ~1,500 (start-date, horizon) windows.
- [Bitcoin vs. Real Estate](https://lastcoinstanding.com/bitcoin-vs-real-estate): The structural relationship between housing costs, incomes, and Bitcoin. The true opportunity cost of buying a house instead of Bitcoin.
- [The Bitcoin Horizon](https://lastcoinstanding.com/the-bitcoin-horizon): Volatility versus risk as different categories. No rolling 41-month or longer Bitcoin window has ever ended in nominal loss.
- [The Bitcoin Fixed Share](https://lastcoinstanding.com/the-fixed-pie): The 21-million supply ceiling and what mathematically-fixed share of a monetary network actually means.
- [The Bitcoin Retirement](https://lastcoinstanding.com/the-bitcoin-retirement): Required stack size at withdrawal age against long-run trend growth. Adjustable assumptions, transparent math, no advice.
- [Disciplined Rebalancing](https://lastcoinstanding.com/disciplined-rebalancing): Percentile-trigger rebalancing applied to fifteen years of price data. Turning the Power Law channel from a model into a protocol.
- [Borrowing Against Your Stack](https://lastcoinstanding.com/borrowing-against-your-stack): The cautionary version of using Bitcoin as collateral — rehypothecation tiers, liquidation distance under stress, interest cost versus retained-coin appreciation. Strong emphasis on the failure modes.
- [Bitcoin-Backed Mortgages](https://lastcoinstanding.com/bitcoin-backed-mortgages): Mortgage scenarios with Bitcoin collateral. Same caution profile as the broader borrowing page.

## Tools

- [The Gallery](https://lastcoinstanding.com/the-gallery): A curated vertical scroll-through of the ten most striking charts across the site — Power Law channel, BTC required to buy a US house, real opportunity cost of buying a house, global housing affordability, BTC needed for retirement, 4-year rolling CAGR vs. the S&P 500, the BTC outperformance heatmap, the half-life of a dollar, and CAGR ranges by holding horizon. Each chart full-size with editorial framing; designed as a one-stop entry point that shows the structural argument from model → consequence → evidence → horizon.
- [The Bitcoin Heatmap](https://lastcoinstanding.com/heatmap): Standalone interactive — bitcoin's outperformance against the S&P 500 and NASDAQ-100 across every monthly entry from 2010 and every common holding horizon, in one grid. Click-to-explore navigates to the full Bitcoin vs. Stock Market article with the cell's parameters pre-loaded.
- [Calculators](https://lastcoinstanding.com/calculators): Central hub linking to every interactive calculator across the site.

## About

- [About](https://lastcoinstanding.com/about): About the site and its author. Long Coin Standing is an independent editorial project; it accepts no advertising and offers no financial advice.
